evidence: AOI normalized from user text to Athabasca Basin margin focus area: Wollaston Lake East / eastern Wollaston area, Saskatchewan. No polygon, claim id, or exact coordinate supplied.
source: GeoMine MCP normalize_aoi plus analyst normalization
evidence_grade: C for geometry; A for jurisdiction after source review
screening_implication: Usable as a regional screen, not as a tenure or drill-target boundary.
key_limitation: No authoritative AOI geometry; MCP did not geocode or infer province.

evidence: Combined eastern Wollaston envelope returned 867 EM conductor features: 830 airborne EM and 37 ground EM; conductor types include VLF-EM, ZTEM, HLEM, MK VI, VTEM, VTEM+, and Input.
source: Saskatchewan GeoAtlas EM Conductors FeatureServer layer 7
evidence_grade: A for conductor presence; D for mineralization inference
screening_implication: Strongly supports a conductor-focused uranium target lane because graphitic basement/shear conductors are relevant to Athabasca uranium targeting.
key_limitation: Layer cautions locations may be 200-300 m off; conductors are not mineralization.

evidence: NRCan/SGS 2023 eastern Wollaston EM map sheets include apparent conductivity, time-decay constant, magnetic field products, and interpretation at 1:50,000 scale.
source: Government of Canada Publications records M183-2/8966E-PDF and M183-2/8967E-PDF
screening_implication: Independent official support for detailed conductivity mapping in the exact eastern Wollaston area.
key_limitation: Maps need sheet-by-sheet interpretation and georeferenced extraction before target ranking.

evidence: Combined eastern Wollaston envelope returned 270 radioactive-boulder points; CPS range 0 to 22000, median 350; dominant assessment files include 64E11-0029 and 64E11-0027.
source: Saskatchewan GeoAtlas Radioactive Boulders FeatureServer layer 4
evidence_grade: A for boulder records; B/C for source-vectoring
screening_implication: Adds a near-surface radioactivity/pathfinder lane that can prioritize conductor segments if glacial transport is understood.
key_limitation: No U3O8 assay values in queried attributes; boulder transport direction and source bedrock are unverified.

evidence: The Saskatchewan Resource Map uranium potential layer intersects the focus envelope.
source: Saskatchewan GeoAtlas Resource Map Uranium Potential layer 11
screening_implication: Regional-scale official map supports uranium favourability at the area scale.
key_limitation: The queried polygon had null descriptive attributes; it is regional prospectivity, not target proof.

evidence: Uranium Deposit Footprints returned 0 overlapping records in the combined eastern Wollaston envelope.
source: Saskatchewan GeoAtlas Uranium Deposit Footprints layer 5
screening_implication: No known mapped uranium deposit footprint was confirmed inside the selected envelope.
key_limitation: Does not rule out occurrences or prospects outside the footprint layer, and does not replace SMDI/SMAD review.

evidence: Live Saskatchewan Analytical and Rock Property Data query returned 0 overlapping records in the focus envelope; local lake-water staging files also contain 0 samples in the same envelope.
source: Saskatchewan Analytical and Rock Property Data FeatureServer layer 0; repo local SGS lake-water files
provenance_type: Live ArcGIS count + local file check
evidence_grade: B for absence in checked layers
screening_implication: Geochemical evidence is currently a data gap for this exact focus envelope.
key_limitation: Absence from checked layer is not evidence of absence; other media and assessment reports may exist.

evidence: CDoGS identifies relevant regional surveys around Wollaston/Athabasca: lake sediment/water survey over parts of NTS 64L/74I/74P; biogeochemical survey over NTS 64L/74I and small parts of 64E/74H; peat/soil/till/vegetation and groundwater/lake datasets mostly west/northwest of Wollaston Lake.
evidence_grade: A for survey metadata; C for direct AOI fit
screening_implication: CDoGS is a priority lane for regional geochemical context and background thresholds, but not yet direct evidence for the eastern Wollaston conductor envelope.
key_limitation: Survey footprints mostly do not cover the selected eastern Wollaston EM-map envelope; analytical data were not downloaded/normalized.

evidence: Nearby eastern Athabasca projects report basement-hosted uranium targeting, thin sandstone/no-cover settings, VTEM/ANT/gravity/conductor targets, alteration, and anomalous uranium drilling.
source: IsoEnergy East Rim and Geiger Energy Wollaston public pages
screening_implication: Supports that the broader eastern margin/Wollaston corridor is actively explored using the same conductor-plus-basement uranium model.
key_limitation: Company pages are not official government occurrence data and require NI 43-101/assessment-report verification.

evidence: MCP infrastructure tool did not calculate distances and identified required inputs: authoritative AOI geometry, CRS, and infrastructure layers.
screening_implication: Infrastructure should remain a separate GIS lane after AOI polygon is resolved.
